MUSE_dezawa.title: Multilineage Differentiating Stress Enduring (Muse) Cells: A New Era of Stem Cell-Based Therapy
MUSE_dezawa.note: Comprehensive review of MUSE cell biology, safety (non-tumorigenic), and clinical trial evidence

umbilical_cord_msc.title: Infusion of Human Umbilical Cord Tissue Mesenchymal Stromal Cells in Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder
umbilical_cord_msc.note: Phase I clinical trial — safe and feasible in young children; 6 of 12 showed improvement on ≥2 ASD measures

umbilical_cord_blood.title: Transplantation of Human Cord Blood Mononuclear Cells and Umbilical Cord-Derived MSCs in Autism
umbilical_cord_blood.note: Combined cord blood + UC-MSC transplantation in autism; CARS score improvements documented

bone_marrow.title: Outcomes of Autologous Bone Marrow Mononuclear Cell Administration Combined with Educational Intervention in ASD — Phase II RCT
bone_marrow.note: Randomized controlled trial — bone marrow MNCs + education vs education alone; 50 patients

adipose.title: Efficacy and Safety of Stem Cell Therapy in Children with ASD: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
adipose.note: Meta-analysis including adipose-derived MSC studies; CARS scores significantly lower in stem cell groups

spinal_cord_derived.title: Intrathecal Injection of Bone Marrow Concentrate in Children with ASD: Retrospective Chart Analysis
spinal_cord_derived.note: Intrathecal delivery of bone marrow concentrate for ASD; retrospective analysis of outcomes

exosomes.title: Mesenchymal Stem Cell-Derived Exosomes for Treatment of Autism Spectrum Disorder
exosomes.note: Intranasal hUC-MSC exosomes restore social ability and reduce stereotyped behaviors in ASD mouse models

fetal.title: Efficacy of Fetal Stem Cell Transplantation in Autism Spectrum Disorders: An Open-Labeled Pilot Study
fetal.note: Pilot study of IV + subcutaneous fetal stem cells in ASD children; ATEC and ABC score improvements at 6 and 12 months

placental.title: Stem Cell Therapy in Autism: Recent Insights
placental.note: Review covering placental MSC sources and their immunomodulatory properties relevant to autism

amniotic.title: Amniotic Fluid and Placental Membranes as Sources of Stem Cells: Progress and Challenges
amniotic.note: Characterization of amniotic-derived stem cells: low immunogenicity, non-tumorigenic, high growth capacity

hematopoietic.title: Human Umbilical Cord Blood Infusions in the Management of Autism Spectrum Disorder
hematopoietic.note: Review of cord blood HSC infusions for ASD including Duke ACT trial results; improvements in social communication and expressive vocabulary
